Trustpilot.review0Quote: I absolutely love TM! I feel so relaxed and grounded in a way I never thought possible. It's surprising that I can experience such a depth of relaxation beyond sleep. TM is so simple yet so profound that everyone can do it. It is my go to anchor daily.
Trustpilot.review1Quote: My only regret is that I didn't do it sooner. Totally life changing. I feel so peaceful and happy.
Trustpilot.review2Title: My daughter and I are very happy meditating daily
Trustpilot.review2Quote: My daughter and I are very happy meditating daily. It has giving us lots of insight and peace. We always look forward for our 2 daily meditations. The instructors are very dedicated and easy to understand.
Trustpilot.review3Title: Very professional, from emails to notifications
Trustpilot.review3Quote: Very professional, from emails to notifications to application. Very friendly, patient and experienced teachers. A lot of good information backed by science and research.
Trustpilot.review4Title: One of the best decisions in many years
Trustpilot.review4Quote: This has been one of the Best decisions I've made in many years. If you are in need of a foundational start to your day, this is for you.
Trustpilot.review5Quote: It's real. It's a way to live peacefully in time that belongs to you.
Trustpilot.review6Title: A definite change after just 3 weeks
Trustpilot.review6Quote: I have noticed a definite change in my anxiety levels only 3 weeks into starting TM.
Trustpilot.heading: What others are saying

Hero.badge: The only technique of its kind

TrustBadges.badge4: No need to stop your thoughts

ForWhom.heading: Who is TM for?
ForWhom.item0Title: You're keeping it together — but inside you're constantly on edge?
ForWhom.item0Description:
• You sleep badly, even though you're exhausted.
• You never really switch off — your mind just keeps going.
• Small things throw you off balance more than you'd like.
• You're longing for real rest, not just distraction.

TM gives you 20 minutes of deep stillness every day — and your nervous system starts to recover in a lasting way.
ForWhom.item1Title: You can feel the stress taking a toll on your body?
ForWhom.item1Description:
• Your blood pressure is too high — or you know it soon will be.
• You wake up in the morning already drained.
• Headaches, tension, poor sleep — it's all connected.
• You want to make a change, but another health project sounds like more effort.

20 minutes sitting comfortably, practising the technique — and your body starts to restore itself.
ForWhom.item2Title: You don't want promises — you want proof?
ForWhom.item2Description:
• You're sceptical about meditation — and rightly so.
• You've tried things before that didn't deliver.
• You want to know: has this actually been studied? With real people and control groups?
• Over 400 studies at universities including Harvard and Stanford — covering blood pressure, cortisol, sleep, and heart health.

TM is the most researched meditation technique in the world. Its effects are documented, not just claimed.
ForWhom.item3Title: Some days you feel empty — even when nothing's really wrong?
ForWhom.item3Description:
• Getting out of bed is hard, even when nothing bad has happened.
• You function on the outside, but inside you don't have the energy for what matters to you.
• You feel like you're constantly holding yourself together — and you don't know how much longer you can.
• Joy comes less often than it should.

TM isn't a substitute for professional support — but many people find that it brings deeper calm, a lighter feeling, and a greater sense of connection to themselves.
ForWhom.item4Title: You want a practical path to inner fulfilment
ForWhom.item4Description: The first stage of enlightenment — complete inner contentment and clarity — is already dormant within each of us. We just need to know how to get there. TM gives you direct, effortless access. We experience our innermost nature: pure bliss. This happens during TM in deep stillness, both physical and mental. It allows the body to regenerate deeply and release accumulated stress. The result: we gradually become more able to live the immense joy within us in everyday life — and to let it grow.

WhyTm.heading: What makes TM unique
WhyTm.subheading: Transcendental Meditation is fundamentally different from other techniques.
WhyTm.benefit0Short: Most meditation techniques require focus or willpower. TM is different: the mind is allowed to settle completely naturally — without any effort. That's why so many people find TM surprisingly easy to learn.
WhyTm.benefit0Expanded: Because the technique is effortless, the mind spontaneously moves toward quieter levels of thought. Meditation happens by itself — not through discipline. That's what makes TM accessible even to people who haven't had success with other techniques.
WhyTm.benefit1Title: Deeper than sleep — real rest for your body
WhyTm.benefit1Short: During TM, the body reaches a state of deep rest that studies show is more profound than sleep — while the mind stays awake and alert. In this stillness, the nervous system releases accumulated stress.
WhyTm.benefit1Expanded: This state is measurably different from relaxation, sleep, or concentration. Many people notice deep stillness, clarity, and fresh energy — even before they consciously feel the difference in daily life.
WhyTm.benefit2Short: Every person carries a reservoir of pure contentment within — an ocean of joy that TM makes accessible, bringing more inner satisfaction and less irritability day to day.
WhyTm.benefit2Expanded: What philosophers, mystics, and sages have described for thousands of years becomes concrete and lasting through TM. And it's not just experiential — it's scientifically measurable.
WhyTm.benefit3Short: Studies show that regular practitioners respond more calmly under stress, make sharper decisions, and think more creatively. This isn't self-suggestion — it shows up in measurable brain activity.
WhyTm.benefit3Expanded: Research shows that TM improves coordination between different areas of the brain. This more integrated functioning has been documented in elite athletes, executives, and creatives who practise TM regularly.
WhyTm.benefit4Title: Release stress — even the deep-rooted kind
WhyTm.benefit4Short: TM doesn't just ease acute stress — it also dissolves pressure that's built up over time. Many people report less anxiety, better sleep, and greater resilience.
WhyTm.benefit4Expanded: TM has been studied for stress-related conditions and trauma processing — including PTSD. The deep physiological rest it produces supports the body's natural self-regulation in ways other relaxation techniques simply can't match.
WhyTm.benefit5Short: Transcendental Meditation is the most researched meditation technique in the world. Areas of study include stress, heart health, brain function, performance, and resilience.
WhyTm.benefit5Expanded: Because TM is taught in a standardised way, it can be studied more rigorously than many other approaches. Findings have been published in journals including the American Journal of Cardiology and the International Journal of Neuroscience.

HowItWorks.heading: How it works
HowItWorks.subheading: From curiosity to daily practice — in four simple steps.
HowItWorks.step0Description: Find out what TM is, how it differs from other techniques, and get all your questions answered. No commitment whatsoever.
HowItWorks.step1Description: The technique is taught one-on-one by a certified teacher — at your own pace, tailored to you. No prior experience needed.
HowItWorks.step2Description: 20 minutes, sitting comfortably, twice a day. No app, no guidance, no focusing — just effortless, independent practice.
HowItWorks.step3Description: With regular practice, the calm carries into everyday life: more energy, more clarity — even in stressful moments.

fokus.label: Focus
fokus.headline1: Clarity can't be forced — it just appears.
fokus.subtitle: TM doesn't declutter your mind — it lets clarity emerge on its own: sharper focus, quieter thoughts.

erschoepfung.label: Exhaustion

erschoepfung.subtitle: TM gives you access to genuine inner stillness — not as a technique, but as a natural state.

angst.headline1: No willpower needed. No concentration required.
angst.subtitle: TM calms your nervous system from within — no willpower, no concentration needed.

WasAndereSagen.heading: What doctors & TM practitioners say
WasAndereSagen.quote0: As a child and teenager I was constantly ill — immune deficiency, frequent hospital stays, always exhausted. A year after I started meditating, that was over. My immune system became remarkably strong, and today, in later life, I'm healthier and more capable than I ever was as a young person.
WasAndereSagen.quote1: Transcendental Meditation as preparation. Because when the mind comes from a place of complete stillness, it has a different kind of focus.
WasAndereSagen.extendedQuote1: I notice a growing sense of development in every area of my life. Even in difficult situations I keep a clear head, and I don't feel myself ageing in any way. At 67 I have as much energy as I did at 40, and I can feel my consciousness continuing to expand.
WasAndereSagen.quote2: Meditation has changed my life. Nothing has opened my eyes the way Transcendental Meditation has. It makes me calm and happy, and brings quiet and peace to my pretty chaotic life.
WasAndereSagen.quote3: TM is great for your health and fits easily into everyday life. Anyone who cares about their wellbeing and quality of life should meditate. Of all the forms of meditation I've tried, TM stands out for its simplicity and effectiveness.
WasAndereSagen.quote4: I haven't found anything more efficient or reliable for dealing with stress and fatigue — or for bringing more joy, vitality, and creativity into life — than TM. It's the foundation of a successful personal and professional life.
WasAndereSagen.extendedQuote4: A special bonus was the opening inward — discovering my inner self, my spiritual dimension. That's an enrichment no amount of money can buy. TM has been the quiet, dependable companion of my life for over 42 years, and it will continue to be.
WasAndereSagen.quote5: And it was a gift for life. It's something you can always come back to.
WasAndereSagen.extendedQuote5: In moments of madness, meditation helped me find moments of stillness — and I'd love for it to offer young people in a restless world a quiet place to retreat to as well.
WasAndereSagen.extendedQuote0: I feel a steady growth in every area of my life. Even in difficult situations I keep my perspective, and I don't feel myself aging in any way. At 67 I have as much energy as I did at 40, and I sense a continuous expansion of my consciousness.
